title: Add Git Anomaly Whitelist and Author Check
author: kublai
problem: Git operation monitor is flagging HIGH severity alerts for routine Claude config changes (commit 6969fe48), wasting investigation cycles and desensitizing the team to real security incidents. Rules lack context awareness: any settings.json edit = HIGH, no whitelist for expected changes, no distinction between human-initiated vs unauthorized modifications.
solution: Add commit author check (downgrade to INFO if known human), file path whitelist (whitelist agents/*/.claude/settings.json), anomaly approval UI (CLI to mark expected changes), and enhanced alert metadata (commit author, message, diff summary). Implement in git-operation-monitor skill.
expected_impact: Reduce false positive rate by ~60%, keep HIGH severity for real incidents, faster triage via enhanced metadata, prevent repeat alerts via approval mechanism, maintain security posture.
risk: LOW (defensive default: still alert, just lower severity for known-safe cases)
success_criteria: Zero HIGH severity alerts for authorized human config changes
All repeat alerts prevented via approval mechanism
security_considerations: Risk: Whitelist could miss real attacks if attacker compromises human git credentials. Mitigation: Keep alerting (downgrade to INFO, don't suppress), require 2FA for human git operations, monitor for suspicious patterns even in whitelisted files, audit whitelist quarterly.
rollback_plan: Remove author check and whitelist logic, revert to original HIGH severity for all settings.json changes, monitor for missed legitimate alerts. Revert time <10 minutes.
